purpose: Identifies the current phase of the AI cycle by monitoring a four-stage cross-asset deterioration pattern with monitorable signposts and historical hit rate of 83%
components: Stage 1: Breadth deterioration — equal-weighted underperformance of 800-1200bp (CURRENTLY ACTIVE)
Stage 2: Secondary beneficiary peaking — 15% underperformance threshold for secondary AI beneficiary basket
Stage 3: Curve signal — yield curve behavior signaling macro stress
Stage 4: Credit widening — IG spreads move from 120-180bp toward historical distressed 300-500bp
Historical lead time: equity leads credit by 6-12 months, 83% hit rate across 6 episodes
current_assessment: Currently in Stage 1 with breadth deterioration active matching 1998 timing. Legacy media equity down 25-30% while IG spreads remain tight at 120-180bp. Credit widening expected H2 2026-H1 2027 if historical lead holds.
limitations: N=3 for fully documented four-stage episodes
Passive credit indexation may distort Stage 4 signal
Self-funded capex bust may alter historical timing patterns
Phase transitions are not deterministic and false signals are possible

purpose: Maps the self-reinforcing feedback loop connecting AI capex, corporate profits, equity prices, capital flows, and the US dollar to identify systemic vulnerabilities
components: AI capex (~$250B) sustains corporate profits via Kalecki identity
Profits sustain equity prices and S&P 500 earnings growth
Equity prices attract international capital inflows ($40-60B/month)
Capital inflows support dollar strength (AI premium 5-8 cents EUR/USD)
Dollar strength supports margins for US multinationals
Margins sustain capex capacity (OCF/capex 1.5-1.7x) and loop restarts
Fiscal deficit floor ($1.8-2T) provides partial insulation against collapse
current_assessment: Loop currently in strengthening mode with all linkages active. Fiscal floor means 25% AI capex cut is only 3-4% of fiscal floor, limiting Kalecki-driven earnings collapse. Key vulnerability is international capital flow persistence.
limitations: AI capex is marginal not dominant driver vs fiscal deficit (10-15x larger)
Dollar-equity beta is qualitative; linear extrapolation was refuted
Loop reversal dynamics are theoretically sound but empirically untested at this scale
Fiscal consolidation could paradoxically weaken the loop through Kalecki channel

purpose: Decomposes hyperscaler ROIC by capital vintage to demonstrate the arithmetic certainty of blended ROIC convergence toward WACC regardless of assumptions
components: Pre-2022 capital (~$600-800B): legacy ROIC 25-35%
2022-2024 AI-era capital (~$350-500B): estimated ROIC 5-10%
2025-2027 incremental capital (~$400-600B): assumed ROIC 3-8%
Blended ROIC weighted by capital stock proportions
WACC reference: 9-11% for hyperscalers
current_assessment: At zero capex growth blended ROIC declines from ~22-25% to ~14-17% by 2028. At 30-50% growth blended reaches ~12-15% approaching 9-11% WACC. Blending arithmetic is mechanical and irrefutable.
limitations: 2022-2024 vintage ROIC has plus/minus 5pp uncertainty due to revenue attribution opacity
Revenue bundling creates irreducible denominator uncertainty
Assumes current capital stock retains productivity; accelerated obsolescence would worsen
Does not account for potential ROIC improvement from learning curve effects

purpose: Establishes the historical base rate for whether infrastructure builders vs users capture primary economic value from General Purpose Technology deployments
components: Six GPT episodes analyzed: 1/6 (17% naive) where builders captured value
Differentiating variable: bottleneck control during deployment phase
Hyperscaler adjustment for vertical integration raises rate to 25-40%
Market-implied probability at 70-80% represents conjunction fallacy
Current prices conflate AI productivity is real with AI investors capture the value
current_assessment: Market prices imply 70-80% value capture probability significantly exceeding the adjusted base rate of 25-40%. Even granting vertical integration as genuinely different, the gap represents a conjunction premium.
limitations: N=6 GPT episodes is a small sample
Each GPT has unique characteristics making direct comparison imprecise
Vertical integration adjustment of 25-40% is subjective
Does not distinguish hyperscalers that both build and use infrastructure

purpose: Categorizes the current AI capex cycle as a self-funded bust distinct from debt-funded busts to predict correction dynamics of slow grind rather than crash
components: Self-funded criterion: OCF/capex greater than 1.0x (currently 1.5-1.7x)
Analogue 1: Japanese corporates 1990s-2000s — TOPIX underperformed ~60% over 13 years
Analogue 2: Energy majors 2010-2014 — P/E compressed 14x to 8x over 2 years
No creditor-imposed stop-loss; discipline is internal or shareholder-driven
Behavioral model: 12-18 months tolerance then 2-quarter compressed capitulation
Phase transition indicators: capex/revenue stalling 3+ quarters, sequential AI revenue decline, management language shift
current_assessment: Current cycle maps to early-to-mid-cycle by both analogues. No phase transition indicators active. Modal outcome is 5-10% annual underperformance compounding over years rather than sharp drawdown. Unresolved whether capital flows create non-linearity absent in analogues.
limitations: N=2 for self-funded bust analogues is thin
Japan had home bias and energy majors had no FX feedback; current cycle has both
US shareholder activism may provide discipline absent in Japanese analogues
Cannot determine slow grind vs sharp correction until initial repricing reveals capital flow persistence

purpose: Maps the three-way constraint the Fed faces during a potential AI bust with above-target inflation to assess the structural impairment of the Fed put
components: Option 1: Intervene in Treasuries creates fiscal dominance concerns especially with basis trade unwinding
Option 2: Cut rates aggressively risks unanchoring inflation expectations starting at 2.5-3.5%
Option 3: Stand pat risks deleveraging cascade through cross-margined positions
Current inflation 2.5-3.5% limits easing to 150-250bp in 6 months vs 550bp in 2001
Historical easing/tightening asymmetry ratio averages 3.5x across 3 episodes
current_assessment: Fed AI put is structurally impaired vs 1998-2001. No clean analogue exists for an AI bust with 3%+ inflation. The 2020 precedent is misleading because Fed could print into deflationary shock. Basis trade adds novel dimension where Treasury intervention itself could be destabilizing.
limitations: Trilemma assumes all three options equally constrained; Fed may find a hierarchy in practice
Inflation might decelerate during AI bust partially relaxing constraint
Historical asymmetry ratio may not apply given novel conditions
Does not account for coordination with other central banks or fiscal authorities

purpose: Provides three independent analytical approaches to measuring AI equity concentration risk that converge on the same conclusion of extreme and underestimated concentration
components: PCA: PC1 explains 45-50% of return variance vs 30-35% historical
ENB via Meucci entropy: momentum ENB at ~25-35 below 2007 and 2020 precedents
Factor correlations: momentum-quality tripled from +0.15-0.25 to +0.50-0.60
Convergence of three independent metrics indicates systematic risk model underestimation
current_assessment: All three metrics at or near historical extremes simultaneously. Multi-factor portfolios holding momentum and quality running ~2x intended AI exposure. Market-neutral portfolios carry ~15-25% unhedged AI directional exposure.
limitations: ENB comparison across time periods faces regime-change uncertainty
PC1 elevation could partially reflect genuine fundamental correlation not just crowding
Factor correlations could persist if AI-driven fundamentals justify co-movement
Three years of post-ChatGPT data too short for definitive factor attribution

purpose: Quantifies cross-asset impact of real rate moves to demonstrate asymmetric positioning risk and the no-good-outcome dynamic for US-centric multi-asset portfolios
components: 50bp real rate rise: Mag-7 -12-15%, S&P 500 -6-8%, IG spreads +10-20bp, DXY +2-3%
50bp real rate decline: equities helped but dollar weakened stressing feedback loop
Effective equity duration: Mag-7 at ~25-30 years at 35-40x forward P/E
No-good-outcome asymmetry: every direction hurts at least one portfolio component
current_assessment: At 2.0-2.2% 10Y TIPS real rates are the cross-asset fulcrum. Rising rates hurt equities directly; falling rates help equities but weaken dollar threatening capex-profit-inflow loop. US-centric portfolios face no safe direction under current conditions.
limitations: Sensitivity estimates subject to plus/minus 30% estimation error
Linear sensitivity may break down for moves greater than 100bp
Correlation regime could shift during large rate moves
Does not account for Fed policy response to rate moves

purpose: Converts vague cycle-phase assessment into practical monitoring indicators for timing the AI cycle transition from expansion to correction
components: Capex/revenue ratio failing to improve for 3+ consecutive quarters
First hyperscaler reporting sequential AI revenue decline
Management language shifting from investment to discipline
Capex/OCF crossing 55-65% for 2-3 hyperscalers (projected late 2027)
Secondary beneficiary basket underperforming by 15%+ threshold
Equal-weighted vs cap-weighted underperformance exceeding 800-1200bp (already active)
current_assessment: Mid-cycle expansion with 1-3 years of runway under modal outcome. Equal-weighted underperformance already active matching Stage 1. No other phase transition indicators triggered. Carry cost of being 12-24 months early on anti-AI positions is the binding constraint.
limitations: Indicators pattern-matched from small sample of prior cycles
Lag between indicator triggering and market repricing is uncertain
Multiple indicators could trigger simultaneously rather than sequentially
Self-funded nature may alter indicator sensitivity vs historical precedents
